bostonglobe-2022.text: When organic is toxic: How a composting facility likely spread massive amounts of ‘forever chemicals’ across one town in MassachusettsState officials say the PFAS likely spread through ground water from the 240-acre composting facility, which has been accepting tens of thousands of tons of organic waste every year and selling it as loam, potting soil, and mulch.

Boris Johnson resigns, remains British prime minister for nowJohnson said it is “clearly the will” of his Conservative Party that there should be a new leader as he announced his resignation. Johnson said he will remain as British prime minister while a leadership contest is held to choose his successor.

Three T derailments in three days. Lots of questions. A curious delay.Three MBTA construction derailments — with no passengers and no injuries — would not be publicly confirmed until the T’s top official faced questions from reporters days later.

Man allegedly assaulted by white nationalist group in Boston calls for investigation into police responseCharles Murrell, who said he was assaulted by the Patriot Front group in Boston on Saturday, is calling for an investigation into why police officers did not intervene and make arrests as the alleged attack was unfolding.
